---
title: "Installation & Setup"
---

## Installing because-py

because-py is the Python NumPyro backend for the R [`because`](https://because-pkg.github.io/because/) package.
It can be used from R (the recommended approach) or directly in Python.

---

## From R (recommended)

### Step 1 — Install the `because` R package

```r
# Stable release
remotes::install_github("because-pkg/because@v1.3.1", build_vignettes = TRUE)

# Latest development version
remotes::install_github("because-pkg/because", build_vignettes = TRUE)
```

### Step 2 — Install reticulate

```r
install.packages("reticulate")
```

### Step 3 — Install Python (if needed)

If you do not already have Python on your system, `reticulate` can install
Miniconda for you (skip this if Python is already installed):

```r
reticulate::install_miniconda()
```

### Step 4 — One-step setup

`because` provides a built-in helper that installs all required Python packages
(`numpyro`, `jax`, `jaxlib`, and `because_py`) into a dedicated, isolated Python environment:

```r
install_because_numpyro()
```

The function automatically creates an environment named `"because_env"`. From then on, R will automatically detect and bind to this isolated environment silently in the background whenever you run a model, completely shielding you from any cross-package contamination.

### Troubleshooting: wrong Python environment

The `because` package automatically binds to `"because_env"` silently in the background. However, if your RStudio setup blocks automatic detection and throws a "Failed to import python module" error, you can manually point R to the correct environment by putting this at the very top of your script (before calling `library(because)`):

```r
# If you installed via standard Python:
reticulate::use_virtualenv("because_env", required = TRUE)

# If you installed via Miniconda/Anaconda:
reticulate::use_condaenv("because_env", required = TRUE)
```

---

## Directly in Python

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/because-pkg/because_py.git
```

Or from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/because-pkg/because_py.git
cd because_py
pip install -e .
```
